data.description:
`rebar show` and `rebar list`/`rebar search` return DIVERGENT compiled-ticket-
state shapes for the SAME ticket, because they run two different reducer
implementations that have drifted. CONFIRMED by code examination AND experiment
(isolated repo + library).

## Divergence (symmetric difference = exactly 2 keys)
- show emits but list/search do NOT: `verify_commands` (conditional, when set)
- list/search emit but show does NOT: `parent_status_uuid`

## Root cause (file:line)
- Dispatcher: `show` -> jq reducer `ticket_show` in ticket-lib-api.sh (initial_state ~341-362 has NO parent_status_uuid; VERIFY_COMMANDS branch ~451-452 adds verify_commands). `list` -> Python `reduce_all_tickets`; `search` -> ticket-search.py -> Python reducer.
- Python reducer: ticket_reducer/_state.py:28 always seeds `parent_status_uuid`; ticket_reducer/_processors.py has NO VERIFY_COMMANDS processor at all (verify_commands never produced).
- So the two reducers (jq for show; Python for list/search/native reduce_ticket — which also backs MCP list_tickets/search) are NOT equivalent despite the jq header claiming "same logic as ticket_reducer".

## Impact
- `verify_commands` is INVISIBLE in list/search/native output even when set on the ticket.
- `parent_status_uuid` (optimistic-concurrency fork marker) is invisible in show output.
- Breaks the "one canonical TicketState schema" plan (story fe3e-346e-e5eb-42db): there is no single shape today.
- tests/interfaces/test_parity.py does NOT catch this — it compares only selected fields (status/title/type/tags/comments/assignee/deps), never full key sets or these two keys.

## Fix
- Unify the two reducers so show == list == search == native reduce_ticket:
  add a VERIFY_COMMANDS processor to the Python reducer (emit verify_commands),
  and add parent_status_uuid to the jq reducer's state — OR make one reducer the
  single source of truth and have the other path call it.
- Add a parity test that compares the FULL top-level key set of show vs a
  list/search element for the same ticket (and with verify_commands set).
- This unblocks/feeds the canonical TicketState schema in the story.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] show, list, search, and native reduce_ticket return identical top-level key sets for the same ticket (incl. when verify_commands is set)
- [ ] verify_commands appears in list/search output when set; parent_status_uuid handling consistent across paths
- [ ] new parity test compares FULL key sets (would have caught this)
- [ ] no regression in existing reducer/parity tests
data.title: show vs list/search return divergent ticket-state shapes (two reducers drifted: verify_commands vs parent_status_uuid)
